differential_diagnoses: Infectious (mycotic) aortic aneurysm with threatened rupture
Aortic dissection (Type B or non-A, non-B)
Aortitis due to S. aureus infection
next_steps: Urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to assess for endocarditis and visualize the thoracic aorta
Repeat CT angiography of the chest/abdomen with contrast to evaluate for progression of the aortic aneurysm and signs of impending rupture
Urgent cardiothoracic and vascular surgery consultation
Continuous cardiac monitoring in an intensive care unit
Blood cultures (repeat set) to document clearance of bacteremia
Strict blood pressure control (e.g., intravenous labetalol or nicardipine) to prevent aortic rupture
Consultation with Infectious Disease for optimization of long-term antistaphylococcal antibiotic therapy
